text:
# Page 1^page=1
Humana Care Management Report – Clinical Status Update Patient Name: Lisa A. English Date: April 2026 Reporting Party: Caregiver (Son) Reason for Update Clinical status update following medication adjustment (addition of potassium) and ongoing home-based care monitoring. Overall Clinical Status Patient is clinically stable and demonstrating measurable improvement in demeanor, engagement, and overall tolerance to current care plan. She shows strong potential for continued improvement with consistent caregiver support and optimized care coordination. Respiratory Status (COPD) 1 Oxygen-dependent at approximately 2.2 L/min via concentrator 2 SpO■ consistently averaging 91–92% at rest 3 Transient desaturation to ~89% noted during exhalation with rapid recovery 4 Respiratory pattern synchronized with oxygen delivery; no acute distress observed Pain / Musculoskeletal Assessment 1 Localized pain primarily in left knee and lumbar/back region 2 Pain appears multifactorial: mechanical (positioning/support) and inflammatory 3 Hydrocodone provides short-duration analgesia; breakthrough pain present 4 Non-opioid OTC agents ineffective per patient report 5 Ongoing positioning adjustments improving tolerance Medication Profile Update

# Page 2^page=2
1 Prednisone discontinued due to lack of sustained efficacy 2 Hydrocodone continued for pain management (short-acting) 3 Potassium supplement initiated (no adverse effects observed to date) 4 Monitoring recommended for electrolyte balance and cardiac-related symptoms Mobility & Functional Status 1 Limited mobility; requires assistance for transfers (bed to commode) 2 Transfer capability present but inconsistent 3 Assistive equipment currently suboptimal (stability concerns noted) 4 Fall risk present but mitigated with caregiver involvement Environmental & Positioning Factors 1 Recent relocation to higher humidity environment 2 Humidity may contribute to perceived discomfort but not currently destabilizing 3 Current sleeping surface contributing to suboptimal spinal alignment 4 Active adjustments in progress to improve positioning and pressure distribution Caregiver Observations 1 Improved patient demeanor and communication 2 Care routines becoming more structured and consistent 3 Increased responsiveness to interventions 4 Care complexity remains moderate but manageable Care Optimization Requests 1 Pain management reassessment (consider duration and consistency of relief) 2 Confirmation of potassium indication, dosing, and monitoring protocol 3 Evaluation for supportive DME (mattress, transfer aids) 4 Optional environmental guidance (humidity management, positioning support) Clinical Summary

# Page 3^page=3
Patient remains clinically stable with improving functional and behavioral indicators. Continued caregiver support, along with targeted optimization of pain management, equipment, and environmental factors, is expected to further improve outcomes. Priority Level Moderate – Stable with active improvement trajectory; supportive optimization recommended.
